answer:
\boxed{A}  

Under the majority view in a lien theory state, a mortgage creates only a lien on the debtor’s interest, not a transfer of title, so the joint tenancy is not severed. When the partner dies, the right of survivorship extinguishes the lien because the deceased’s interest vanishes, leaving the surviving joint tenant with unencumbered title. Thus, the bank cannot execute on the mortgage after the debtor’s death.
